data.description:
Normalize all eyebrow/label/meta patterns to canonical 'text-sm font-medium tracking-widest uppercase' (delivered via .text-eyebrow / .text-label / .text-meta utilities). Eliminate the drift: ~12x text-xs vs ~8x text-sm, weights ranging across font-semibold, font-thin, unlabeled.

Per-site judgment: which sites are eyebrows (above a title), which are labels (forms/UI), which are meta (timestamps/credits/attribution). Spec captures the semantic distinction — visual rendering is identical for now.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Eyebrow is the most-drifted pattern in the audit. Three sizes and three weights for what should be one chrome voice. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: All small uppercase mono labels go through .text-eyebrow, .text-label, or .text-meta. No remaining manual 'text-xs tracking-widest uppercase font-semibold' soup in src/. Plan file records per-site semantic assignments.
data.title: Eyebrow + label + meta normalization — adopt chrome utilities

data.body:
**Cold review by claude-opus-4-7 (verdict: PASS).**

Branch frac-54-eyebrow-normalization @ 15bea94. Reviewed without writing the code; pulled in fresh.

## Mechanical verification

- **Diff shape:** 13 files, 18 insertions / 18 deletions (matches plan).
- **Migration sites verified (15/15):**
  - Eyebrows (8): Events.tsx, Projects.tsx, SectorHeader.tsx, DocumentBadge.tsx, StoryPage.tsx, NeighborhoodPage.tsx, Hero.tsx group label, LabPage.tsx Research+Writing — all use `.text-eyebrow` with preserved unrelated modifiers (color/opacity/margins, inline house tints).
  - Labels (4): Campus Photo, Hero search input, Hero result row, Navbar mobile menu — all use `.text-label`.
  - Meta (3): Hero "No results", Footer "New York City" locale, Footer "New York City Collective" tagline — all use `.text-meta`.
- **Inline overrides correctly preserved:** SectorHeader/DocumentBadge/StoryPage retain `style={{ color }}` (house tints — critical for wayfinding).
- **Inline overrides correctly dropped:** Footer.tsx:80 inline `fontFamily: 'JetBrains Mono'` removed (utility provides via `--font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace`); Navbar.tsx:412 inline `fontSize: 14px` and `letterSpacing: 0.08em` removed (utility provides `text-sm` and `letter-spacing: 0.1em`).
- **Density override:** Hero.tsx:235 dropdown group label keeps `text-[10px]` on top of `.text-eyebrow` with explanatory comment as planned.
- **TODO breadcrumb:** Home.tsx:35 has `{/* TODO FRAC-56: mono uppercase body block — flag for body-cleanup task */}` above the body block; className unchanged.
- **Test update:** neighborhood.test.tsx assertion changed from `tracking-widest` → `text-eyebrow`; test passes.

## Grep audits (all clean)

- `rg "text-xs.*font-semibold.*tracking" src/` → zero matches.
- `rg "text-sm.*font-semibold.*tracking" src/` → zero matches.
- `rg "text-eyebrow|text-label|text-meta" src/` → exactly 15 matches in source (8/4/3 split as planned), 1 in updated test.
- `rg "fontFamily.*JetBrains" src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` → zero.
- `rg "letterSpacing.*0\.08" src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx` → zero.

## Scope discipline (clean)

- Zero changes to src/index.css, src/components/ui/button.tsx, DESIGN.md (FRAC-51 territory).
- Zero changes to shadcn ui/*, fake-button CTAs, JustifiedParagraph blurbs, HouseBannerGrid, PretextLabel, FractalObject, OctahedronHero, BadgePlayground.
- Zero hero/heading size changes (FRAC-55 territory).
- Zero `normal-case` removals (FRAC-56 territory). Body block on Home.tsx left intact with TODO.

## Validation

- `pnpm build` → OK, 3.60s, no warnings beyond pre-existing chunk-size note.
- `pnpm typecheck` → OK, zero errors.
- `pnpm test` → 143 pass / 4 fail. The 4 failures are exactly the pre-existing baseline:
  1. footer.test.tsx — "should render branding text with italic styling" (FRAC-88 regression, pre-existing).
  2. footer.test.tsx — "should use Jacquard 24 font for the branding" (FRAC-88 regression, pre-existing).
  3. navigation.test.tsx — "mobile nav with abbreviated labels for long section names" (pre-existing).
  4. neighborhood.test.tsx — "min-h-screen flex centering" (pre-existing, unrelated to SectorHeader).
  The neighborhood SectorHeader "should display the SectorHeader with letter V" test (the one updated for FRAC-54) PASSES.
- `npx @google/design.md@0.2.0 lint DESIGN.md` → 0 errors, 27 warnings (pre-existing unused-color warnings), 1 info.

## PRD alignment

Re-read .lattice/plans/FRAC-22.md. FRAC-54 is pure typography normalization, consistent with the PRD's wayfinding goal (house labels in SectorHeader/DocumentBadge/StoryPage retain inline tints, so house identity preserved). Mobile-first not regressed. No contradictions.

## Sanity checks

- Hero search input: border `border-foreground/20`, bg `bg-background/90 backdrop-blur-sm`, placeholder, focus `focus:border-foreground/40 focus:text-foreground/80`, h-[30px], pl-8 pr-3 — all preserved verbatim. Still a real input.
- LabPage "Research + Writing": `not-italic` removal is correct (the new utility uses `@apply font-mono` which has no italic; the legacy class had no italic source either, so `not-italic` was a no-op defensive override). No regression.
- Footer tagline dropping `fontFamily: JetBrains Mono` is safe — verified `--font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace` is the only mono token (index.css:8).

## Visual judgment (cold reading)

These three deltas are real and acceptable per the plan's escalation notes:

1. **Footer tagline tracking** went from `tracking-[0.25em]` to `letter-spacing: 0.1em` (utility value). That's a 2.5x tightening, noticeable on the wide "NEW YORK CITY COLLECTIVE" tagline. The plan explicitly accepted this. My take: looks fine for consistency. If Jules wants the wider tracking back later, it's a one-liner `tracking-[0.25em]` add-on. Don't block on this.

2. **Footer "New York City" locale** size went from `text-xs md:text-sm` to uniform `text-sm`. A small (1px-ish) growth on mobile only. Negligible.

3. **LabPage "Research + Writing"** weight went from `font-thin` (100) to `font-medium` (500) — a substantial weight bump. Plan explicitly accepted this as "the kind of drift to fix." JetBrains Mono at 500 will look noticeably more present than at 100. Cold reading: this is intentional consistency cleanup. Visible but not a clash with the LabPage hero treatment (which uses a separate display font). Acceptable.

## Minor observation (non-blocking)

The plan describes the Hero.tsx:235 dropdown group label color as `text-foreground/30`, but both the original code and the new code use `text-foreground/40`. Plan-document inaccuracy, not an implementation bug. The implementation preserved what was actually there. Worth a one-line plan-doc fix if anyone cares, but does not affect the work.

## Verdict

**PASS.** Clean, mechanical, well-scoped sweep. Every migration aligns with the spec, every preservation is honored, every documented out-of-scope site is untouched, and every accepted visual delta was explicitly flagged in the plan. Build/typecheck/tests/DESIGN.md lint all green. Recommend moving to done.
